description:
SUPERSEDED by the fz-ul4.27.22.8..16 ticket chain.

After investigation with the user, this work was decomposed into atomic steps under fz-ul4.27.22:

- .8  fz-cps-seam.closure-lit: Descr::closure_lit variant + ops
- .9  fz-cps-seam.resolve-return: closure-aware return resolution helper
- .10 fz-cps-seam.makeclosure-emit: MakeClosure types as closure_lit
- .11 fz-cps-seam.codegen-direct: codegen lowers TailCallClosure to direct return_call
- .12 fz-cps-seam.force-drop: drop closure_target_fns tagged-return seed (subsumes .6)
- .13 fz-cps-seam.union-singletons: direct dispatch for union of closure_lits (P4)
- .14 fz-cps-seam.retire-fn-constants: retire parallel fn_constants
- .15 fz-cps-seam.retire-halt-kind-plumbing: reassess fc1ff766 plumbing
- .16 fz-cps-seam.retire-uniform-forces: audit + retire residual forces

Design: closure values get singleton types in Descr (mirrors int_lit / atom_lit). Per-callsite specialization falls out of standard spec-key discovery. Per-spec direct dispatch at codegen replaces the indirect cl+16 path when the closure Var resolves to one FnId. Force-drop is then safe because every body's ABI matches its dispatch path.

Close this ticket once .12 lands.
title: fz-cps-seam.arg: closure body arg-slot narrowing via per-callsite arg-type keys
